title: Chromatic gateway CW corpus
scope: Forty-two named CW spaces spanning calibration examples, torsion cofibers, projective and lens filtrations, Lie and Schubert spaces, cyclic and elementary-abelian classifying spaces, complex orientations, and a universal Thom space. Finite-type formulas are materialized only through degree 24; the snapshot computes ordinary homology and does not claim Morava K-theory or chromatic type calculations.

references.title: H infinity Ring Spectra and Their Applications, Chapter V
Low codimensional embeddings of Sp(n) and SU(n)
Generalized group characters and complex oriented cohomology theories
Complex Cobordism and Stable Homotopy Groups of Spheres

families.label: Point
families.summary: The terminal one-cell CW complex and zero-dimensional calibration object.
families.chromatic_relevance: A baseline for reduced versus unreduced conventions and contractible-space behavior.

families.label: Spheres
families.summary: The basic finite CW complexes whose suspension spectra organize stable and chromatic homotopy.
families.chromatic_relevance: The sphere spectrum is the unit and primary target of chromatic stable-homotopy calculations.

families.label: Homology spheres
families.summary: A triangulated Poincare homology sphere, deliberately paired with S^3 to prevent identity-by-homology.
families.chromatic_relevance: It is an identity and model-integrity guard: ordinary homology agrees with S^3 while the fundamental group does not.

families.label: Wedges
families.summary: A simple attaching-map control with the same cellular chain ranks as CP^2 but a different homotopy type.
families.chromatic_relevance: It guards against treating cellular homology matrices as complete CW identity data.

sources.locator: Chapter 0, wedge sums and CW constructions

families.label: Surfaces
families.summary: The torus and Klein bottle provide orientable, nonorientable, free, and 2-torsion two-dimensional test cases.
families.chromatic_relevance: These common topology spaces stress nontrivial 2-cell attaching words and distinguish zero cellular boundaries from degree-two boundaries.

sources.locator: Examples 0.8 and 2.36-2.37, cell structures and cellular homology

families.label: Real projective spaces
families.summary: Finite skeleta of BC_2 with one cell in every degree and visible 2-primary torsion.
families.chromatic_relevance: Real projective filtrations are standard inputs to 2-primary stable homotopy, image-of-J, and vector-field calculations.

families.label: Hopf projective planes
families.summary: The complex, quaternionic, and octonionic projective planes retain the eta, nu, and sigma attaching maps.
families.chromatic_relevance: These three Hopf-invariant-one cofibers are foundational finite complexes whose identical-looking zero cellular differentials hide different attaching maps.

families.label: Moore spaces
families.summary: Degree-m two-cell cofibers isolate torsion at chosen primes, prime powers, and degrees.
families.chromatic_relevance: After stabilization, prime Moore spaces supply V(0), the first finite torsion input to the chromatic type filtration.

families.label: Lens spaces
families.summary: Weighted odd-dimensional cyclic sphere quotients with explicit primary torsion and identity-sensitive weights.
families.chromatic_relevance: Finite lens filtrations approximate BC_p; the two 5-primary three-spaces deliberately share homology but not homotopy type.

families.label: Stunted projective spaces
families.summary: Quotients retaining consecutive real or complex projective cells.
families.chromatic_relevance: Stunted projective complexes are classical finite inputs to stable homotopy, K-theory, vector-field, and image-of-J calculations.

sources.locator: Examples 0.4, 0.6, and 2.42, using the CW quotient filtration

families.label: Compact Lie groups
families.summary: Low-rank SU and Sp examples with finite CW decompositions and nontrivial attaching maps.
families.chromatic_relevance: Compact Lie groups are core generalized-cohomology inputs and expose finite complexes whose cellular boundaries alone omit essential attaching data.

families.label: Schubert spaces
families.summary: A complete flag variety and Grassmannian with explicit even Schubert-cell ranks.
families.chromatic_relevance: Schubert filtrations supply geometric, complex-oriented test spaces beyond projective space while remaining finitely computable.

sources.locator: Section 2.2.1, Flag manifold and Schubert cells, pp. 53-54
Section 1.2, Grassmannian Schubert CW structures, pp. 31-34

families.label: Cyclic classifying spaces
families.summary: Infinite lens spaces BC_p with finite-type CW filtrations and recurring p-torsion.
families.chromatic_relevance: Complex-oriented theories see the formal-group p-series on BC_p; these are direct chromatic character-theory inputs.

families.label: Elementary-abelian classifying spaces
families.summary: Products B(C_p^r) with explicit tensor-product CW filtrations and parameterized p-torsion ranks.
families.chromatic_relevance: Elementary abelian p-groups are central test groups in generalized character theory, chromatic fixed points, and detection.

sources.locator: Product CW structures and Kunneth formula, Sections 0 and 3B

families.label: Infinite projective spaces
families.summary: The universal complex and quaternionic line spaces with even finite-type CW filtrations.
families.chromatic_relevance: CP-infinity equals BU(1) and is the universal complex-orientation/formal-group-law test object; HP-infinity supplies the quaternionic analogue.

sources.locator: Example 0.6, p. 7, and Example 4.50, p. 388: CP-infinity cells and K(Z,2)
Proposition 3.10 and preceding discussion, p. 89: G_1(C^infinity)=CP-infinity classifies complex line bundles
Quaternionic projective cells, p. 222; Examples 4.46 and 4.50, pp. 386 and 388: S^3 -> S^infinity -> HP^infinity

families.label: Unitary classifying spaces
families.summary: BU(2) via the infinite complex Grassmannian and its partition-indexed Schubert cells.
families.chromatic_relevance: BU(2) is the first universal complex-bundle base with two Chern classes and the base for the universal rank-two Thom space.

families.label: Universal Thom spaces
families.summary: The Thom space of the universal complex 2-plane bundle over BU(2), represented by its shifted Schubert filtration.
families.chromatic_relevance: This is the actual space commonly denoted MU(2), one of the spaces assembled into the complex-cobordism spectrum underlying the Adams-Novikov and chromatic stories.
